title: A polynomial Freiman-Ruzsa inverse theorem for function fields

def:1.title: Generalised arithmetic progressions
def:1.summary: GAPs over $\mathbb{F}_q[t]$ — the target structure for inverse theorems.

thm:1.summary: PFR for function fields: If $|A+tA| \le K|A|$ then $A$ covered by $K^{O(1)}$ translates of GAP of rank $O(\log K)$.

thm:2.summary: Vector space case: weak/structural/strong arithmetic dimensions all equal $k$.

thm:4.summary: Optimal lower bound for $|A+\xi A|$ when $\xi$ transcendental over $\mathbb{F}_p[t]$.

conjecture:1.summary: Conjecture on higher-parameter bounds with multiple transcendental dilates.

edges.note: The function-field PFR theorem rests on the vector-space dimension characterization.
Theorem 3 gives the self-contained proof of the dimension equality.
The dimension characterization uses the technical Lemma 1.
Applying the main theorem to transcendental dilates yields the optimal sumset lower bound.
